summary: Comprehensive testing of special ordination dose feature (whole/half pills). All features working correctly: dose inputs in ordination modal, dose persisted to database, dose displayed on Dashboard and Schedule, backend stock calculation using stored dose, PDF generation working.
passed_tests: BACKEND: Existing schedule entry has whole=2, half=1 in special_ordination
BACKEND: Create schedule with custom dose (3 whole, 2 half) saves correctly
BACKEND: GET /api/schedule returns dose fields in special_ordination
BACKEND: PUT /api/schedule updates dose fields correctly
BACKEND: Stock calculation uses stored dose (2.5 pills/day for 2+1*0.5)
BACKEND: Medicine days_until_empty calculated correctly (Stock 95 / 2.5 pills = 38 days)
BACKEND: PDF endpoint returns valid PDF
BACKEND: Cron endpoint executes successfully (91 users processed, 23 medicines updated, 1 email sent)
BACKEND: Cron uses stored dose for daily pill calculation
FRONTEND: Dashboard shows '2½ Piller 125mcg' for special ordination entry
FRONTEND: Dashboard shows 'Special ordination' label on medicine items
FRONTEND: Schedule page shows 'Special ordination: 2½ Piller · 1. mar. — 31. dec. 2026 · Hver dag'
FRONTEND: Edit modal shows ordination summary with 'Antal: 2½ Piller'
FRONTEND: Ordination modal loads existing dose values (Hele: 2, Halve: 1)
FRONTEND: Ordination modal has whole/half input fields with data-testid
FRONTEND: Ordination modal shows calculated total dosage (125mcg)
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: calculate_daily_pills correctly uses ord_data.get('whole', 1) and ord_data.get('half', 0) as defaults
Backend: PDF generation line 1040 uses ord.get('whole', 1) and ord.get('half', 0) for dose
Backend: Schedule entry response includes special_ordination with whole/half fields
Frontend Schedule.js: ordData state properly includes whole/half fields initialized to 1/0
Frontend Schedule.js: ordination modal has ord-whole-input and ord-half-input with data-testid
Frontend Schedule.js: Save button correctly stores whole/half in special_ordination object
Frontend Dashboard.js: Line 66 uses entry.special_ordination.whole/half instead of hardcoded 1/0
seed_data_creation: Existing test schedule entry with special_ordination: {start_date: '2026-03-01', end_date: '2026-12-31', repeat: 'daily', whole: 2, half: 1}. Medicine: Levothyroxin 50mcg, unit: piller, stock: ~95
context_for_next_testing_agent: Special ordination dose feature fully implemented and verified. The ordination modal has whole/half inputs (data-testid: ord-whole-input, ord-half-input). Dose is stored as {whole: N, half: M} in special_ordination object. Dashboard shows dose as '2½ Piller' format. Schedule list shows 'Special ordination: 2½ Piller · date range · repeat'. Stock calculation uses stored dose correctly. PDF generation uses stored dose. Cron endpoint working with email notifications.

features_verified.ordination_form_dose_inputs: PASS - Hele/Halve inputs visible with correct data-testid
features_verified.pdf_generation_uses_stored_dose: PASS - PDF endpoint returns valid PDF
features_verified.cron_email_notification: PASS - Sent 1 email for low-stock medicines
